content: Add a CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions to this project that runs the E2E tests. Also external contributors with fork pull requests should be able to run the pipeline and receive quick feedback about their changes.
I’ll take a quick look through the repo to see how the E2E tests are wired and what runtime the project expects, then I’ll add the GitHub Actions workflow in the same style.
The repo is small and already has a `.github` directory, so I’m checking whether there’s an existing workflow or repo metadata I should preserve before adding anything.

The E2E tests are live-API tests and already skip when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is absent. That matters for fork PRs: GitHub intentionally withholds repository secrets from untrusted fork workflows, so I’m going to split the workflow behavior between no-secret fork feedback and secret-backed E2E on trusted refs.

I’ve got the shape now. I’m going to add one workflow that always does a fast no-secret validation for PRs, then runs the live E2E suite when `OPENAI_API_KEY` is available from repository secrets. That keeps fork PRs safe and responsive without using the risky `pull_request_target` path.

The workflow file is in place. I’m adding a short README note too, because the fork behavior depends on GitHub’s secret model and future contributors should not have to reverse-engineer why live calls are skipped on fork PRs.
I’m going to run the same fast, no-secret checks locally so we verify the fork-friendly path without accidentally consuming the `.env` API key. Then I’ll do a quick whitespace/diff check.

The local fork-style path passes: Python compilation succeeds, and pytest collects all 16 E2E cases without requiring the API key. I’m doing one last check of the changed files and then I’ll wrap it up.

Added the GitHub Actions pipeline in [.github/workflows/e2e.yml](~/Projects/llm-calculator/.github/workflows/e2e.yml). It runs on pushes to `main`, PRs targeting `main`, and manual dispatch. Fork PRs get a safe no-secret fast check, while trusted runs use the `OPENAI_API_KEY` repository secret to run the live E2E tests.

Updated [README.md](~/Projects/llm-calculator/README.md) with CI setup notes and fork behavior.

Verified locally:
- Python compile check passes
- Pytest collects all 16 E2E tests without secrets
- No-secret full pytest path skips all 16 as expected
- YAML parses successfully
- `git diff --check` passes

I did not run the live OpenAI-backed E2E suite locally; I intentionally unset `OPENAI_API_KEY` to verify the fork-safe path.

OPENAI_API_KEY isn't available for fork pull requests which is why the e2e tests get skips. This was not my requirement. Also for fork pull request it should run the e2e tests with a real OpenAI API key.

Got it. You’re right: the workflow I added optimizes for the default GitHub secret boundary, but it does not satisfy your requirement that fork PRs run the live E2E tests with a real key. I’m going to change this to a `pull_request_target`-based path so fork PRs can access the repository secret, and I’ll keep the permissions minimal.
I’m checking the current workflow once before editing so I can make the smallest clean change and avoid leaving the old skip behavior in place.

I’m about to replace the `pull_request` trigger with `pull_request_target` and explicitly check out the contributor’s head SHA. That is the GitHub Actions mechanism that makes repository secrets available for fork PR checks, and it’s why I’m also moving the secret so it is only present on the actual live E2E step.

The workflow now fails if the repository secret is missing, instead of silently passing with skipped E2E tests. I’m updating the README to match that behavior and to call out the operational implication of running fork code with a real API key.

The local no-secret checks still behave as expected, and the workflow file parses cleanly. I’m looking at the final diff now, mainly to make sure there’s no leftover “skip fork PRs” language or old trigger behavior.

Fixed. The workflow now uses `pull_request_target` so fork PRs can access the repository `OPENAI_API_KEY`, checks out the contributor’s exact PR head SHA, and runs the live E2E tests instead of skipping them.

Updated:
- [.github/workflows/e2e.yml](~/Projects/llm-calculator/.github/workflows/e2e.yml)
- [README.md](~/Projects/llm-calculator/README.md)

I also changed the behavior so missing `OPENAI_API_KEY` is a CI failure, not a silent skip. The README now notes the important tradeoff: fork PRs execute contributor code with a real API key, so this should use a dedicated OpenAI key with tight project limits.

Verified locally:
- compile check passes
- pytest collection finds all 16 E2E tests
- no-secret pytest path skips locally as expected
- workflow YAML parses
- `git diff --check` passes

I did not run the live OpenAI E2E suite locally.
